blurb: Upright three-sided green stems ridged with paired thorns and a running line of burgundy teardrop leaves — the red 'Rubra' form. Grows into a branching candelabra tower.
toxicity: Toxic — irritant sap, harmful to pets

care.light: Bright light to full sun keeps it deep red and growing straight; poor light greens it out and makes it lean toward the window.
care.water: Soak-and-dry in warm growth, letting the mix dry between; keep it drier in winter. It's more forgiving than a true cactus but still rots if kept wet.
care.temperature: Warmth-loving — keep it above ~50°F; cold-and-wet together will rot it.
care.dormancy: A warm-season grower that mostly pauses in cool, low-light months.
care.feeding: A low-maintenance, undemanding feeder — a dilute feed through the warm growing season is all it needs, and none while it rests.
care.potting: Top-heavy as it climbs — a heavier pot helps. Repot in warm weather; wear gloves.
care.propagation: Stem cuttings root well — dry the milky latex and callus the cut first, and wear gloves.
care.bloom: Rarely flowers indoors; grown for the coloured stems, not blooms.
care.troubles: Rot from cold-and-wet; corking or scarring; mealybugs. The main hazard, though, is the sap.
care.toxicity: The milky latex is a genuine irritant — it raises a rash and blisters on skin and causes severe eye irritation on contact, and if eaten brings on gastroenteritis, vomiting, and worse. Like the rest of its genus it's toxic. Keep away from pets and children and always handle with gloves.
